title: festuca glauca var. laevigata
intro: Festuca glauca var. laevigata, also known as blue fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and acting as a natural antioxidant. Festuca glauca var. laevigata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
